mnemonics: PUP: Plan for Usage Peaks. A puppy needs to grow into its paws, so plan IT capacity for future growth and peak usage.
keyPoints: Performance measures how well a system does work, capacity is the maximum work it can handle.
Capacity planning involves forecasting future demand to ensure adequate resources.
Key metrics include response time, throughput, and utilization.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on current usage and not forecasting future growth.
Over-provisioning resources (wasting money) or under-provisioning (causing bottlenecks).
Ignoring the relationship between utilization and performance degradation (e.g., the 'knee' of the curve).
